summary: Phase 9 — Interior Design Service e2e testing complete. Backend: 11/11 new pytest tests passed in /app/backend/tests/test_phase9.py (2.56s) covering: (a) GET /api/design/eligibility for client@ returns eligible:true, properties[].rooms[] with 5 rooms (Living/Dormitor/Bucătărie/Baie/Hol), concept_price_per_room=2200.0, max_token_discount_pct=50, available_tokens; (b) eligibility for specialist@ → 403; (c) eligibility for fresh client (no twin) → eligible:false reason:'no_twin_unlocked'; (d) POST /api/design/concept-request with invalid room_ids → 400 'Camere invalide'; (e) tokens_to_use=99999 capped at 50% (1100 for 1 room, 2200 RON full_price → 1100 RON final_price), tokens actually deducted from user (verified via /auth/me); (f) created request appears in GET /api/requests with category='interior_design' and design_concept{rooms, rooms_count, full_price, tokens_used, final_price, style_preference}; (g) specialist accepts lead, then POST /api/design/phase-quote appends {phase_name, description, price, estimated_days, status:'pending'} to request.phases[]; (h) POST /api/design/phase-accept?request_id=X with quote_id deducts client wallet_balance, marks phase status='paid'; oversized quote → 400 'Sold insuficient'; (i) POST /api/design/phase-complete?request_id=X with quote_id releases 95% to specialist wallet (verified delta on /auth/me); (j) REGRESSION old /api/services/interior-design/* (availability/order/quotes) all return 404. Phase 8 regression: 18/18 still pass (3.74s) — no regressions. Frontend Playwright: (1) Client logged in → InteriorDesignCard visible with 'design-open' button enabled (twin unlocked path); (2) Modal opens with all 5 rooms toggleable + 'Selectează toate' works; 8 style chips present (Modern/Scandinavian/Minimalist/Industrial/Boho/Clasic/Rustic/Japandi); notes textarea fills; token slider max correctly equals min(tokens, 50% price) — for 1 room shows max=1100; live recalc shows 2200 RON - 1100 RON = 1100 RON; submit 'Plasează cererea' closes modal and creates request (6 interior_design requests with 'Faze design' buttons now visible in list); (3) Clicking 'Faze design' opens DesignPhasesViewer 'Faze proiect design' showing FAZA CONCEPT summary (Camere: 1, Preț: 2200 RON, Tokeni: -1100, Final: 1100 RON, Stil: modern); (4) Specialist login shows 6 'Acceptă (45 RON)' Design Interior leads in Oportunități Noi + 1 'Propune fază nouă' button on in_progress interior_design job (matches expected flow). Server-enforced pricing model (2200 RON/cameră fixed, 50% token cap) holds end-to-end through frontend ↔ backend integration.

minor.issue: Endpoint allows specialist to create unlimited follow-up phase quotes once status='in_progress' — no rate-limit or cap; acceptable for MVP but worth a soft cap (e.g., max 10 pending quotes per request) to prevent spam.
If client passes duplicate room_ids (e.g., same uuid twice), rooms_count is computed as len(data.room_ids) — so duplicates double-bill. Consider de-duping via set() before pricing.

Money is deducted from wallet_balance but no audit lock — concurrent calls on the same quote_id could both pass the status='pending' check before the $set runs. Use findOneAndUpdate({phases.id, phases.status:'pending'}) atomic update to eliminate race.

action_items: Optional: de-duplicate room_ids server-side in /design/concept-request to avoid double-billing on accidental duplicate IDs.
Optional: atomic findOneAndUpdate on phase-accept to prevent concurrent double-debit on the same quote.
Optional: soft cap on pending phase quotes per request (e.g., 10) in /design/phase-quote.
critical_code_review_comments: DESIGN_CONCEPT_PRICE_PER_ROOM and DESIGN_MAX_TOKEN_DISCOUNT_PCT are module-level constants (2200.0 / 50) — clean single source of truth; eligibility endpoint exposes both to FE so server is the only authority on pricing. ✓

tokens_to_use is correctly server-clamped: tokens_to_use = max(0, min(data.tokens_to_use, int(max_token_discount), user.get('tokens', 0))) — cannot exceed 50% cap nor user's current balance, even if FE sends 99999. ✓
design_concept payload is snapshotted onto the request (rooms[], rooms_count, full_price, tokens_used, final_price, style_preference) — immutable history preserved even if twin rooms later change. ✓
Phase quote IDs use uuid4() rather than ObjectId — appropriate for embedded array elements (avoids ObjectId serialization friction). ✓
Phase-complete pays 95% to specialist via $inc on wallet_balance and writes a 'design_phase_payout' transaction — consistent with the existing job-completion flow.

POSSIBLE RACE on phase-accept: read request → check phases.$.status=='pending' → $set status='paid' is non-atomic. Low risk for MVP (single client clicking accept) but a 1-line fix to use findOneAndUpdate with a filter on phases.status.
server.py is now 2566 lines (+~94 vs phase 8) — still the elephant in the room; splitting into routers/ (auth, marketplace, design, twin, admin) remains an open phase-10+ candidate.
Old /services/interior-design/* endpoints fully removed (404 confirmed) — no dead handlers left in the routing graph. ✓

Frontend uses data-testid on every interactive element (design-card, design-open, design-locked, design-modal, design-prop-{id}, select-all-rooms, design-room-{id}, design-style-{id}, design-notes, design-token-slider, design-submit, phases-{id}, design-phases-viewer, propose-phase-modal, phase-desc, submit-phase, accept-phase-{id}, complete-phase-{id}) — excellent testability.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — InteriorDesignCard, InteriorDesignModal (all 5 rooms + 8 styles + slider cap + live recalc), DesignPhasesViewer, and Specialist 'Propune fază nouă' button all render and function correctly. Modal submission and request listing integration verified end-to-end.

test_credentials: client@propmanage.io/Client123!, specialist@propmanage.io/Spec123!, admin@propmanage.io/Admin123!, operator@propmanage.io/Op123! — all working. Note: client's tokens drift between runs as tests deduct; if exhausted, db.users.updateOne({email:'client@propmanage.io'},{$set:{tokens:3500}}).
seed_data_creation: test_phase9.py creates per-run: 1 fresh TEST_phase9_nt_* client (no twin, for eligibility:false check), and several interior_design requests on client@propmanage.io ('TEST_phase9 cap test', 'TEST_phase9 no tokens', 'TEST_phase9 phase flow'). Each phase-flow run pays a 800 RON quote from client wallet → 760 RON to specialist wallet. ~1100 tokens consumed per pytest run via the cap test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 9 (Interior Design Service) is fully green. The server-enforced pricing model (2200 RON/cameră, 50% token cap) is correct and matches FE display. The phase quote/accept/complete cycle correctly moves money: wallet → escrow (paid) → 95% specialist wallet on complete. Old /services/interior-design/* endpoints are gone (404). Phase 8 regression still 18/18. Phases 1-7 not re-run but their critical paths (login, requests CRUD, accept lead, escrow, disputes, twin editor, admin analytics) are exercised transitively through phase 9 (specialist accept + start, client confirm, wallet/escrow operations). server.py is at 2566 lines — overdue for a routers/ split. The 6 interior_design requests now in client's list from earlier phase-9 runs will accumulate over re-runs — fine for now but consider a teardown that deletes TEST_phase9_* requests if test suite is run repeatedly.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. The three 'minor' backend issues listed (duplicate room_ids double-billing, phase-accept race condition, no cap on phase-quote count) are defense-in-depth hardening suggestions — none are reproducible in the current single-user MVP usage pattern. All 11 new pytest cases and all 18 phase-8 regression cases pass cleanly; full frontend integration (client modal → backend → request list → phases viewer → specialist propose-phase button) works end-to-end.
